repo_notes.content: Audience and voice: this wiki serves two readers, in this order — (1) the maintainer (Calvin) returning after months away, who needs invariants, gotchas, why-decisions, and where-to-change-what fast; (2) recruiters and peer engineers evaluating the engineering practice (zero-JS architecture, test-gated deploys, CSS-only animation). Always prefer explaining WHY a decision was made and WHAT breaks if violated — and which test or comment guards it — over restating what the code does: the 'what' is a handful of small .astro files anyone can read in minutes. The primary sources are the in-repo why-comments (netlify.toml header, the index.astro grid comment, the uno.config.ts safelist and inline-block comments, test-file doc blocks) — quote them with line anchors rather than paraphrasing.

Architecture ground truth (treat contradictions as errors): the production site is fully static — astro.config.mjs sets output: 'static'; no adapter, no server runtime, no middleware. dist/_astro contains exactly one CSS file and zero .js files, and no serverless/edge function is emitted — enforced by the literal test names 'emits exactly one stylesheet', 'ships zero external JavaScript files', and 'emits no Netlify serverless or edge function' in tests/build-output.test.ts. The page ships zero <svg>: every icon is a UnoCSS presetIcons CSS mask rule. Total first-party client JS is two tiny inline scripts: the pre-paint theme resolver in src/layouts/BasicLayout.astro and ThemeSwitcher's toggle listener. The only external script is third-party Umami analytics, whose tag is emitted unconditionally — the UMAMI_ID env var only populates its data-website-id attribute; do not describe the script as conditionally rendered. Svelte, the motion library, the @astrojs/netlify SSR adapter, and astro-icon were all deliberately DELETED in past improvement runs — treat any trace as historical, never document them as present.

Upstream-contamination warning: this repo is a fork of Ladvace/astro-bento-portfolio that has diverged substantially, and prior auto-generated docs blended upstream and pre-refactor knowledge into descriptions of this repo. Specifically: Card (src/components/Card/index.astro) has ONLY the props {title?, titleIcon?, colSpan?, rowSpan?} — no href mode, no arrow-up hover, no colorText/height/padding/bgColor (features of the deleted pre-refactor Card). ProgressBar.astro contains ZERO JavaScript — no IntersectionObserver, no scroll-triggered animation anywhere in current source (the term survives only in archived plans/done/ documents describing the deleted Svelte component); it animates with a pure-CSS progress-grow keyframe on page load. The fork's whole identity is having deleted that JavaScript — re-derive every claim about interactivity from current source.

Content-pipeline facts every page must get right: src/lib/constants.ts is the single source of every user-facing string; its exports are LINKS, CAREER, ABOUT_ME, GOALS (plural — a Goal[] array; there is NO singular GOAL export; the Goal type's progress_last_year is number|null, where null means 'no comparable figure' and renders as a dash), WELCOME, NOW, FOOTER, and METADATA. Icons stored in constants are Iconify ids ('collection:name') converted to UnoCSS classes solely by iconClass() in src/lib/icons.ts — the one mapping components and the uno.config.ts safelist must share; the single exception is ThemeSwitcher's sun/moon icons, literal i-ri-* classes written in the component source that UnoCSS extracts directly and that never touch iconClass() or the safelist. METADATA.email_obfuscated is defined and test-gated but never rendered by any component — do not list it as page content.

Settled decisions — present as decisions with their recorded rationale, never as gaps, bugs, or TODOs for the reader: the Netlify UI build-command field is deliberately EMPTY while publish=dist is deliberately ALSO set in the UI (netlify.toml header explains both traps); build plugins are UI-installed only; GitHub Actions was offered and declined (plans/README.md — the Netlify build command IS the CI); constants.ts sits outside the eslint glob (test-gated instead); @typescript-eslint/parser looks unused but is resolved at runtime by eslint-plugin-astro — removing it breaks linting; no Playwright (deliberate for two lines of client JS); og:url is origin-only BY DESIGN while canonical and METADATA.site_url carry a trailing slash — never assert or describe them as equal; public/llms.txt is hand-synced with constants.ts by maintainer choice; Strava progress automation and the maintainer's self-description copy are deliberately left to him.

plans/ is a decision ledger, not source code. plans/README.md is the living index; its execution table with per-plan commit hashes is the AUTHORITATIVE status source — its prose header has demonstrably lagged its own table (the header said plans 011–014 were TODO after the table marked 011 DONE). Read statuses from the table at generation time: never present a TODO plan as shipped, never document an archived plan's pre-change description as current architecture, and take the next plan number from the index (numbering is monotonic across runs). plans/done/README.md archives per-plan verification evidence (mutation-tested assertions, preview-vs-production diffs). Rejected findings are recorded WITH refutation evidence precisely so they are never re-derived.

Currency rule: derive every quantitative claim — test/assertion counts, dependency counts, component counts, the number of improvement runs, goal km figures, byte sizes, plan statuses, commit hashes, the stagger-ladder length vs <main> child count — from the current code at generation time, never from README, memory, or plans prose. This repository is a continuous work in progress: page titles deliberately avoid frozen counts, and generated prose must never introduce one that will rot. This repo has documented history of numbers rotting in prose: a 3000km-vs-5000km description drift shipped to production (which is why constants.test.ts now forces METADATA.description to mention each goal's target figure), and the improve-run prompts carried stale numbers twice. Anchor each claim to the file and lines it comes from.

Scope and skip list: ignore dist/, node_modules/, .netlify/, .scratchpad/, .claude/, .husky internals beyond the one-line pre-commit, and pnpm-lock.yaml. README.md owns getting-started/quickstart — link to it, never duplicate a second setup guide. CLAUDE.md is agent instructions, not human documentation subject matter. public/llms.txt is a hand-maintained AI-crawler bio — mention the manual-sync obligation (a stale job title shipped once) but do not build pages from its content. This is a small single-page site (count source files at generation time if a figure is needed): generate EXACTLY the specified pages — no per-component pages, no glossary, no contributing/roadmap/FAQ boilerplate. Package management is pnpm only (packageManager is pinned; npm install ignores pnpm-lock.yaml).

pages.title: Overview: A Zero-JavaScript Portfolio
pages.purpose: Orient both audiences in one read: calvin.sg is a single-page bento-grid portfolio built on Astro + UnoCSS with output: 'static', no adapter, and no client framework, deployed to Netlify. Frame the engineering identity as subtraction, and lead with the thesis that each removal is 'locked, not just done' — enforced by an assertion, not asserted in prose. Present the headline decisions as a compact before/after table: fully static output replacing an SSR build that served the page from a 2.4MB Netlify function (plan 002, commit 32071fe); zero external JavaScript files replacing a Svelte+motion client runtime (plan 003, commit 621dd5a); zero <svg> — icons are CSS mask rules (plan 006; locked indirectly by build-output.test.ts's per-icon mask-rule assertions — there is no explicit no-<svg> test, so do not claim one); CSS-only animation; a deploy gated by the test suite itself (netlify.toml: 'pnpm check && pnpm test'). These change-commit hashes were verified in git history at authoring time and history does not rot — do not reconcile them against the plans/README.md execution table, which records completion status and whose per-plan hash can be a different commit. Cite 'ships zero external JavaScript files' and 'emits no Netlify serverless or edge function' as literal test names in tests/build-output.test.ts. Enumerate what client code remains: the pre-paint theme script in src/layouts/BasicLayout.astro, ThemeSwitcher's toggle listener, and the always-emitted Umami analytics tag. Note the fork lineage (Ladvace/astro-bento-portfolio, substantially diverged). Include one mermaid diagram of the system: src/lib/constants.ts → the constants-consuming components (derive the exact list from src/components imports at generation time — not every component consumes constants) → prerendered dist/ → test suites → Netlify deploy. Close with a documentation map — README.md owns setup (link, don't restate), plans/ is the decision ledger (see this wiki's Engineering Log page), and this wiki's Maintainer Playbook page is the first stop after time away.

page_notes.content: Keep this page short and diagram-led — it is the recruiter landing page. The removed stack (Svelte, motion, SSR adapter, astro-icon) appears only as the 'before' column with plans/ citations, never in present tense. 'Engineering Log' and 'Maintainer Playbook' are cross-references to sibling pages of this wiki, not repository files.

pages.title: Maintainer Playbook: Invariants and Change Recipes
pages.purpose: The page future-Calvin opens after six months away. Structure as task-oriented recipes, each naming the exact files and the trap that fires, cross-linking the system pages for mechanism detail: (1) Bump goal progress — edit GOALS[n].current_progress in src/lib/constants.ts; constants.test.ts enforces progress finite and within [0, total_goal], and METADATA.description must mention each goal's target figure (the drift gate added after a 3000km-vs-5000km description drift shipped). (2) Add or remove a card — the lg grid packs completely full (quote the current index.astro inline comment for the exact cell arithmetic at generation time; bug history in PR #42): re-balance lg row/col spans in IntroCard/AboutMe/Now, AND extend the nth-child stagger ladder in BasicLayout.astro — a lockstep test in build-output.test.ts pins the ladder to the card count (plan 013), so a missing rung fails the deploy. (3) Add a social link — append to LINKS; its icon is auto-safelisted because uno.config.ts maps over LINKS; but an icon in a NEW constants field silently renders as nothing until the safelist maps over that field too. (4) Change job/role — CAREER[0] drives JSON-LD worksFor/jobTitle in BasicLayout; also hand-update public/llms.txt (nothing keeps it in sync; a stale title shipped once). (5) Iterate on tests — SKIP_BUILD=1 pnpm test reuses dist/. Then a hard 'do not helpfully fix' list with evidence links: the empty Netlify UI build command (netlify.toml header); @typescript-eslint/parser (runtime-resolved by eslint-plugin-astro — removal breaks linting, refuted in plans/README.md); constants.ts outside the eslint glob (test-gated instead); og:url origin-only vs canonical trailing slash; monotonic plan numbering (read the next number from plans/README.md); pnpm only, never npm.

page_notes.content: Recipes intentionally restate invariants owned by system pages, but as one-line trap + link, not full mechanism. Verify each recipe's file references against current source at generation time.

pages.title: Architecture: One Page, One Bento Grid
pages.purpose: Document the full render path and component contracts. src/layouts/BasicLayout.astro wraps the single page src/pages/index.astro and builds the entire <head> from constants: OG/Twitter tags, canonical, a JSON-LD Person schema (sameAs filtered to absolute LINKS URLs, worksFor/jobTitle from CAREER[0]), and the deferred Umami script tag — emitted unconditionally, with the UMAMI_ID env var populating only its data-website-id attribute. It also carries the load-bearing inline <script is:inline> that resolves theme from localStorage/prefers-color-scheme and stamps <html data-theme> before first paint, while the static markup hard-codes data-theme="light" so a no-JS visitor gets the full designed palette instead of transparent cards (colors are CSS custom properties scoped to :root[data-theme]). The bento grid: md 2-col; lg 4-col × 8-row with lg:min-h-[736px] (short laptops scroll instead of clip) and lg:max-h-[800px]; quote the index.astro inline comment verbatim and re-read it at generation time, because the packing arithmetic changes whenever cards change — at authoring time the lg grid was packed exactly 32/32 cells, and adding a card without re-balancing lg spans in IntroCard/AboutMe/Now lets auto-placement push the Now card into a zero-height implicit row and clip it (shipped as a real bug, fixed in PR #42). Give a compact component roster table derived from src/components at generation time, not subpages; contracts verified at authoring time include: Card is the only primitive with props {title?, titleIcon?, colSpan?, rowSpan?}; Goal renders generically from the Goal type (generalized in PR #41 for cycling + running); ProgressBar carries a full ARIA progressbar contract — aria-valuemax is the real km target, not 100, and aria-valuetext composes human-readable figures; IntroCard renders the portrait via astro:assets Image with densities=[2]; ThemeSwitcher is the ONLY interactive component. Icons are aria-hidden throughout, with sr-only companion text exactly where an icon is the sole content (the footer 'love' span, LINKS/Strava buttons, ThemeSwitcher's 'Toggle Theme'); icon-beside-visible-text spots (Card's titleIcon, IntroCard's greeting, ProgressBar's goal icons) deliberately carry no sr-only twin. State explicitly: no scroll-triggered animation and no IntersectionObserver anywhere in current source — the term survives only in archived plans/done/ documents describing deleted code.

page_notes.content: Verify every prop and ARIA claim against current source — this repo's own pre-refactor Card had href/colorText/bgColor props, and its deleted Svelte ProgressBar (removed in plan 003) used an IntersectionObserver; neither exists in the current fork and prior auto-generated docs described both as present.

pages.title: Content as Typed Data: constants.ts
pages.purpose: Explain the repo's core pattern: src/lib/constants.ts as a typed, test-gated CMS. Walk each export and its consumers: LINKS (social buttons incl. /resume.pdf, JSON-LD sameAs), CAREER (Career card + JSON-LD worksFor/jobTitle), ABOUT_ME, GOALS (Goal[] — cover the type, especially progress_last_year: number|null where null means 'no comparable figure', rendered as a dash), WELCOME, NOW, FOOTER, METADATA (title/description/site_url/preview image/locality; note email_obfuscated is defined and test-gated but never rendered — intentional, see the rejected finding in plans/README.md). Explain src/lib/icons.ts: iconClass() maps 'collection:name' Iconify ids to UnoCSS classes, and BOTH components and the uno.config.ts safelist must go through it. The page's story is the gating decision: constants.ts sits outside the eslint glob BY DESIGN, and tests/constants.test.ts is its validator — installed icon collections only (fa6-brands, ri), finite goal numerics within [0, total_goal], absolute vs root-relative URL shapes, SEO title/description length bounds, and the flagship drift gate requiring METADATA.description to mention each goal's target figure (a 3000km-vs-5000km drift shipped once — that is the why). Diagram the flow: constants.ts → components, forking into the in-process Container render asserted by tests/rendered-html.test.ts and the prerendered dist/ asserted by tests/build-output.test.ts. Derive which exports rendered-html.test.ts actually asserts from its imports and assertions at generation time — never assume full coverage or a specific gap (a NOW/Career-dates gap existed until plan 014 closed it). Close with the referenced static assets: src/assets/me.webp (portrait, emitted at 1x/2x), public/resume.pdf, public/preview.jpg (og:image/twitter:image), favicon.ico, and public/llms.txt with its manual-sync obligation.

page_notes.content: The export is GOALS (plural array) — a previous config repeatedly said 'GOAL', a nonexistent symbol. Read current goal figures from the file at generation time; never freeze km numbers into prose.

pages.title: Styling, Theming, and the Icon Pipeline
pages.purpose: Document the coupled UnoCSS systems whose failure modes are silent. Icons: presetIcons turns @iconify-json/fa6-brands and @iconify-json/ri ids into CSS mask rules — zero <svg>, zero icon fonts. Quote the two load-bearing uno.config.ts comments: (1) icon classes are derived from constants at render time so UnoCSS never sees them literally — the safelist maps over LINKS logos, GOALS cta_logo AND goal_logo, CAREER icons, WELCOME.greeting_icon, and FOOTER.icon via the shared iconClass() helper; any icon class derived from constants that the safelist does not cover gets no CSS rule and renders as nothing, silently (the one exception: ThemeSwitcher's sun/moon icons are literal classes in component source, extracted by UnoCSS directly, needing no safelist); (2) presetIcons({scale: 1, extraProperties: {display: 'inline-block'}}) — without inline-block the icon <span> stays an inline box, width/height are ignored, and every icon renders at zero size. Diagram the coupling: constants.ts → iconClass() → {components, safelist} → build-output.test.ts asserting every safelisted class emits a rule with both a mask url and inline-block. Theming: color tokens are CSS custom properties under :root[data-theme='light'|'dark'] in BasicLayout — NOT UnoCSS theme tokens (uno.config's theme adds only boxShadow.custom '2px 2px 0', used by Button and ThemeSwitcher, and gray.300). Walk the flash-free flow: pre-paint script stamps data-theme → variables switch → ThemeSwitcher toggles and persists to localStorage, with its sun/moon icons selected purely in CSS via a descendant selector that deliberately outranks presetIcons' display rule (quoted comment in ThemeSwitcher.astro). Then the full CSS-only motion inventory: the card-in entrance keyframe with the hand-enumerated nth-child 0.08s stagger ladder over <main>'s children (check the ladder against the rendered card count at generation time — GOALS.map in index.astro and CAREER.map in Career.astro each emit one card per entry, so count entries, not JSX children; a lockstep test in build-output.test.ts pins ladder coverage to the card count since plan 013), ProgressBar's progress-grow keyframe (1.5s ease-in-out, 0.3s delay, backwards fill) growing to a --progress inline custom property, and Pulse's animate-ping — all load-time. card-in and progress-grow carry prefers-reduced-motion opt-outs; Pulse's animate-ping is the stock presetWind3 utility with NO reduced-motion gate — report it as the one un-gated animation, do not paper over it. Note the second silent-failure class: UnoCSS generates nothing for typo utilities with no warning — enumerate the reintroduction gates from build-output.test.ts at generation time — at authoring time, the DEAD_CLASSES source scan plus the plan-012 gate requiring every class token in the shipped HTML to have a stylesheet rule.

page_notes.content: This page owns the icon-safelist and animation invariants; the Maintainer Playbook links here from its recipes. Anchor every claim to lines in uno.config.ts, src/lib/icons.ts, BasicLayout.astro, ThemeSwitcher.astro, ProgressBar.astro, Pulse.astro, or tests/build-output.test.ts (the safelist mask/inline-block assertions and the DEAD_CLASSES gate).

pages.title: Test Harness: A Green Build Is Not Evidence
pages.purpose: The strongest showcase page, framed by the doc-block principle from tests/build-output.test.ts: assert on rendered HTML and real dist/ artifacts, never on exit codes. Cover each vitest suite under tests/ and what it uniquely proves — enumerate the suites at generation time rather than assuming their number; those present at authoring time were: tests/rendered-html.test.ts renders index.astro in-process via Astro's experimental Container API, parses with linkedom, and asserts the constants reach the page — derive the asserted export set from the suite's imports at generation time (plan 014 closed the former NOW/Career-dates gap) — plus JSON-LD shape, the ARIA progressbar contract, aria-label/sr-only consistency, and icon reference counts. tests/constants.test.ts gates the data layer (see the Content page — it is constants.ts's ONLY validator). tests/build-output.test.ts opens real dist/ files: exactly one stylesheet and zero external JS, no SSR function output, the unicode-range emoji gate locking plan 011's migration (llms.txt deliberately excluded), a mask-url + inline-block rule for every safelisted icon class, the DEAD_CLASSES source gate, the class-to-rule pairing and stagger-lockstep gates from plans 012–013, and the sharpest assertion — a sharp-based pixel decode proving the portrait's 2x srcset candidate carries exactly twice the layout width, because Astro silently DROPS densities that would upscale and only decoding pixels catches the regression (originated from a real production PageSpeed finding). Surface the mutation-testing lessons recorded as comments IN the tests: icon references are counted rather than distinct classes because deleting one class kept the per-class version green; goal figures are matched as composed phrases because bare numbers also appear in prose. Harness mechanics for the returning maintainer: tests/setup/build.ts globalSetup runs one build (SKIP_BUILD=1 reuses dist/) and clears stale .netlify/v1 SSR output first — quote its in-file comment VERBATIM rather than paraphrasing; its point is that 'no SSR function was emitted' is only meaningful about the current build, and a checkout that ever built with the old SSR adapter would otherwise keep that directory forever, failing locally while passing in CI — while preserving .netlify/state.json; vitest.config.ts uses getViteConfig from astro/config and must keep unocss packages in server.deps.inline or the Container render breaks; and the linkedom quirk — dist/index.html's hoisted <script> above <html> leaves document.body empty, so assert text via string toContain and elements via querySelector. Repeat the warning: og:url is origin-only by design, never compare to canonical.

page_notes.content: Do not hardcode assertion counts — read them from the suites at generation time. Quote real test names and in-file comments with line links; they are better documentation than any paraphrase and the credibility anchors for the recruiter audience.

pages.title: Deploy Pipeline: netlify.toml Is the CI
pages.purpose: Explain why this repo has no GitHub Actions and does not need it. Quote the netlify.toml header comment block nearly in full — the most important why-comment in the repo — covering its two deliberate asymmetries: the UI build command is intentionally EMPTY because Netlify resolves the toml over the UI and having both once left a stale-looking dashboard value (netlify api getSite reports it — a trap; verify with 'netlify build --dry'); publish=dist is intentionally ALSO set in the UI as a fail-loud backstop — if the toml were deleted, an empty publish dir would make Netlify serve the repository root, while dist (gitignored) fails loudly instead. Build plugins (netlify-purge-cloudflare-on-deploy, @netlify/plugin-lighthouse) are intentionally UI-installed, not toml-declared, per the same block. Diagram the deploy-gate sequence: git push → Netlify reads netlify.toml → 'pnpm check && pnpm test' → vitest globalSetup runs astro build → the suites gate the deploy (build-output.test.ts asserts on the real dist/; rendered-html.test.ts renders in-memory via the Container API; constants.test.ts gates the data layer) → publish; a failing assertion fails the deploy and production keeps serving the previous build. Frame the CI trade-off honestly: GitHub Actions was offered and declined (plans/README.md, run-1 DX-01 — which also records that the finding's cited 'type error reached production' commit actually showed the deploy failing safely); one pipeline instead of two that can disagree. Local gates: .husky/pre-commit runs lint-staged (eslint --fix on src/**/*.{js,astro}); flat eslint.config.js with eslint-plugin-astro + jsx-a11y recommended sets; tsconfig extends astro/tsconfigs/strict and its **/* include sweeps in the root configs so pnpm check covers them; UMAMI_ID is the only env var, set in the Netlify dashboard, never inlined in the build command. Also own the crawler surface: @astrojs/sitemap derives URLs from astro.config.mjs's site value, public/robots.txt hand-codes the sitemap URL, and build-output.test.ts is what keeps astro.config's site coupled to METADATA.site_url. Close with the parity property: static output with no adapter means pnpm preview serves a dist/ byte-identical to production — the basis for the preview-vs-production diffs logged in plans/done/README.md.

page_notes.content: Never describe the empty UI build command as a misconfiguration or recommend a second CI system — both are recorded decisions with trap rationale in netlify.toml and plans/README.md.

pages.title: Engineering Log: The plans/ Decision Ledger
pages.purpose: The single history page: summarize plans/ as the repo's decision ledger and a working example of governed agentic maintenance, so every other page describes only current state. This page must stay evergreen — the ledger grows run by run, so derive the plan list, per-plan statuses, and completion hashes from the plans/README.md execution table at generation time (authoritative for status; its own prose header has demonstrably lagged it before), and derive the run count and run grouping from the dated per-run headings in the index's 'Findings considered and rejected' section and each plan file's 'Planned at' line — the execution table itself carries no run column. Pull per-plan verification evidence from plans/done/README.md. Structure the narrative as: (1) the arc so far, grouped by run — milestones already in the ledger worth surfacing if still accurate include the regression safety net landing before any refactor, prerendering to static output and deleting the SSR adapter (before which the page lived inside a 2.4MB serverless function), deleting the Svelte+motion client runtime in favor of CSS, replacing astro-icon with presetIcons CSS masks, and the emoji-to-icon migration locked by the emoji-output gate — but always re-verify each against the table and never present this list as exhaustive or current; (2) the governance mechanics, with equal weight, because they are standing policy rather than history and the real differentiator: findings killed by a per-finding adversarial skeptic pass are recorded WITH refutation evidence so no future run re-derives them (examples recorded in the index: removing @typescript-eslint/parser would break linting since eslint-plugin-astro resolves it at runtime; 'add GitHub Actions CI' was maintainer-declined and its own cited evidence showed the deploy failing safely; Playwright judged disproportionate for the amount of client JS involved); the 'Deliberately not planned' section records the maintainer's explicit calls, not oversights — enumerate its current items from the index rather than assuming a count (the llms.txt hand-sync obligation is recorded elsewhere in the index, not in that section); numbering is monotonic across runs, with the next plan number read from the index; the verified-baseline table must be re-checked each run because 'Every failure in the last run came from a plan believing something about the repo that had stopped being true' (quote it exactly, including 'about the repo'); and done/README.md's done-bar is mutation-tested assertions plus preview-vs-production byte diffs. Present all of it as decisions, never as defects or TODOs for the reader.

page_notes.content: Statuses, completion hashes, and the next plan number are volatile — read them from the plans/README.md execution table at generation time; the run count and grouping come from the index's dated run headings and the plan files' 'Planned at' lines, not the table. Never cap the narrative at a fixed number of runs, never present a TODO plan as shipped, and never describe pre-change architecture from archived plans as current.
